notes.standard_1: File is a Python hook script, not a .md command/agent file. Standard 1 (no-inline-code) applies only to .claude/commands/*.md and .claude/agents/*.md. Not applicable.
notes.standard_2: Line 144 contains Path(f"/tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-{session_id}.json"). The path prefix is a stable system-wide naming convention used identically in hooks/lib/allowlist.py:140, 209, 283. The session_id component is fully parameterized. Per Standard 2 'What is NOT a violation': constants that genuinely never change across environments. No violation.
notes.standard_3: No bare python3 invocations in .sh or .md files. The file's shebang (#!/usr/bin/env python3) is a Python hook entry point, outside Standard 3 scope (which targets bare python3 calls in shell/markdown files).
notes.standard_4: No step numbering in this Python file. Not applicable.
notes.standard_5: Filename posttool-allowlist-consume.py is descriptive and specific — names the hook type (PostToolUse), subsystem (allowlist), and action (consume). No forbidden naming patterns.
notes.standard_6: No Chinese characters in hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py. Unconditional scan of all agents/*.md and commands/*.md (and .claude subdirs) found no reportable violations. (Note: agents/style-inspector.md contains documented exempt canonical labels — not a reportable violation per Standard 6 exemption.)
notes.standard_7: posttool-allowlist-consume.py has a specific and unique PostToolUse purpose (sentinel grant consumption). No overlapping hook script exists.
notes.standard_8: File is a Python script, not a .md file. Docstring is functional code documentation explaining design rationale, sentinel contract, and exit semantics. Not verbose/bloated.
notes.standard_9: Hook reads all values from stdin (tool event JSON payload) and environment variables (CLAUDE_TASK_ID, CLAUDE_SESSION_ID). No fixed configuration values requiring parameterization.
notes.standard_10: All imports verified: lib.allowlist exists with all referenced symbols (consume_grant_for_posttool, consume_sentinel_grant_on_terminal_result, match_sentinel_grant_for_write, match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command, load_sentinel_grant_for_task, _enumerate_sentinel_grant_files). The previously-used lib.subagent.is_subagent_context import was correctly removed in this diff. No dangling references.
notes.standard_11: No unresolved merge conflict markers (<<<<<<, >>>>>>, =======) found.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 1 potential violation and 2 observation-only items. Finding 1 (Standard 2, critical): Codex argued that line 144's /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-{session_id} prefix duplicates a convention from lib/allowlist.py and should use a shared helper. Finding 2 (observation): Standard 6 wording is overbroad — exempt mascot labels exist in agents/style-inspector.md but are not reportable. Finding 3 (observation): Standard 3 wording should clarify the Python shebang is out of scope.

findings.issue: Line 144 hardcodes /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist- prefix rather than calling a shared helper from lib/allowlist.py
findings.proposed_fix: Standard 2 violation, critical: hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py:144 duplicates the hardcoded legacy allowlist path prefix instead of using a single source of truth. Move path construction into hooks/lib/allowlist.py, e.g. legacy_grant_path(session_id) -> Path or unlink_legacy_grant_for_session(session_id), and call that helper.
findings.rationale: Standard 2 'What is NOT a violation' explicitly exempts constants that genuinely never change. The /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist- prefix is a stable system-wide naming convention (not an environment-specific value), used identically in lib/allowlist.py:140,209,283. The session_id variable IS parameterized. Refactoring to a shared helper would improve DRY but is not a Standard 2 violation per the defined test: the value does not 'reasonably differ between runs or environments'. Codex's proposed fix describes a code quality improvement, not a style standards violation — separate cycle if desired.

findings.issue: Standard 6 rationale in draft is overbroad — agents/style-inspector.md has documented exempt CJK labels
findings.proposed_fix: Reword: No reportable English-only violation. hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py has no CJK text. The broader agents/commands scan has only documented exempt mascot-label hits, not reportable violations.
findings.rationale: No Standard 6 violation exists. The standard_6 notes field has been updated to reflect the exemption.

findings.issue: Standard 3 wording — 'No python3 invocations found' is imprecise given the #!/usr/bin/env python3 shebang
findings.proposed_fix: Reword: No disallowed bare python3 invocation in .sh/.md files. The Python hook shebang is not within Standard 3's invocation scope.
findings.rationale: No Standard 3 violation exists. The standard_3 notes field has been updated to clarify the shebang exemption.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Observations 2 and 3 incorporated into notes wording. Finding 1 (Standard 2 path duplication) rejected as out-of-scope for style standards — the value meets the 'genuinely never changes' exemption. Codex's proposed refactoring is a code-quality improvement suggestion, not a style violation per the defined standards.
